flour.name: All-Purpose Flour
flour.explanationNote: All-purpose flour can vary by brand; we use the standard 120g per cup (spooned and leveled). Don't scoop directly from the bag — spoon into your cup and level off for consistent results.

bread-flour.name: Bread Flour
bread-flour.explanationNote: Bread flour is slightly denser than all-purpose flour due to its higher protein content. Spoon and level for best accuracy.

cake-flour.name: Cake Flour
cake-flour.explanationNote: Cake flour is lighter and finer than all-purpose. It weighs about 114g per cup when spooned and leveled.

whole-wheat-flour.name: Whole Wheat Flour
whole-wheat-flour.explanationNote: Whole wheat flour is denser than white flours because it contains the bran and germ. Spoon and level for accuracy.

almond-flour.name: Almond Flour
almond-flour.explanationNote: Almond flour (blanched, finely ground) is lighter than wheat flours. Pack gently — don't compress — when measuring by cup.

coconut-flour.name: Coconut Flour
coconut-flour.explanationNote: Coconut flour is very absorbent — you typically need much less than wheat flour. It weighs about 128g per cup.

self-rising-flour.name: Self-Rising Flour
self-rising-flour.explanationNote: Self-rising flour includes baking powder and salt. It weighs about 125g per cup, similar to all-purpose flour.

sugar.name: Granulated Sugar
sugar.explanationNote: Granulated white sugar has a density of about 12.5 grams per tablespoon. Use level tablespoons (not heaped) for best accuracy.

brown-sugar.name: Brown Sugar
brown-sugar.explanationNote: Brown sugar is typically measured packed. One packed cup weighs about 220g. Light and dark brown sugar weigh the same.

powdered-sugar.name: Powdered Sugar
powdered-sugar.explanationNote: Powdered (confectioners') sugar is very light and fluffy. Sift before measuring for consistent results — one unsifted cup is about 120g.

honey.name: Honey
honey.explanationNote: Honey is dense and sticky. One tablespoon weighs about 21g. Spray your measuring spoon with oil first so the honey slides off easily.

maple-syrup.name: Maple Syrup
maple-syrup.explanationNote: Pure maple syrup is slightly less dense than honey. Use a liquid measuring cup for larger amounts.

coconut-sugar.name: Coconut Sugar
coconut-sugar.explanationNote: Coconut sugar has a coarser texture than white sugar. It weighs about 180g per cup — a bit lighter than granulated.

butter.name: Butter
butter.explanationNote: One standard stick of butter is 113g (1/2 cup or 8 tablespoons). Use the markings on the wrapper for easy measuring.

milk.name: Milk
milk.explanationNote: Whole milk; skim and low-fat milk have very similar density. Use a liquid measuring cup for accuracy.

heavy-cream.name: Heavy Cream
heavy-cream.explanationNote: Heavy cream (heavy whipping cream) is slightly lighter than milk by volume due to its fat content.

cream-cheese.name: Cream Cheese
cream-cheese.explanationNote: Cream cheese density can vary by brand. One standard block (8 oz) is about 226g. Soften before measuring by volume.

sour-cream.name: Sour Cream
sour-cream.explanationNote: Sour cream weighs about 230g per cup. Stir gently before measuring for a consistent scoop.

yogurt.name: Yogurt
yogurt.explanationNote: Plain yogurt (regular or Greek) weighs about 245g per cup, similar to milk. Greek yogurt may be slightly denser.

condensed-milk.name: Condensed Milk
condensed-milk.explanationNote: Sweetened condensed milk is thick and heavy at about 306g per cup. One standard can (14 oz) is about 397g.

olive-oil.name: Olive Oil
olive-oil.explanationNote: Olive oil is lighter than water — about 13.5g per tablespoon. Use a liquid measuring cup for larger amounts.

vegetable-oil.name: Vegetable Oil
vegetable-oil.explanationNote: Vegetable oil (canola, sunflower, etc.) weighs about 218g per cup. Most neutral cooking oils have similar density.

coconut-oil.name: Coconut Oil
coconut-oil.explanationNote: Coconut oil is solid at room temperature. Melt it first for accurate measuring by volume, or weigh for best results.

lard.name: Lard
lard.explanationNote: Lard is slightly lighter than butter. Soften to room temperature before measuring by volume for consistency.

rice.name: Rice
rice.explanationNote: Uncooked long-grain white rice; short-grain and brown rice may differ slightly. Always measure before cooking.

rolled-oats.name: Rolled Oats
rolled-oats.explanationNote: Old-fashioned rolled oats are light and airy at about 90g per cup. Quick oats are slightly denser.

cornstarch.name: Cornstarch
cornstarch.explanationNote: Cornstarch (cornflour in the UK) is very fine and powdery. Spoon into your measuring cup and level off — don't pack.

breadcrumbs.name: Breadcrumbs
breadcrumbs.explanationNote: Dry breadcrumbs weigh about 108g per cup. Panko breadcrumbs are lighter; fresh breadcrumbs are heavier.

cornmeal.name: Cornmeal
cornmeal.explanationNote: Medium-grind yellow cornmeal weighs about 157g per cup. Fine-grind cornmeal may be slightly denser.

quinoa.name: Quinoa
quinoa.explanationNote: Uncooked quinoa weighs about 170g per cup. Rinse before cooking to remove the bitter saponin coating.

couscous.name: Couscous
couscous.explanationNote: Uncooked regular couscous weighs about 173g per cup. Israeli (pearl) couscous is heavier.

almonds.name: Almonds
almonds.explanationNote: Whole almonds weigh about 143g per cup. Sliced or slivered almonds are lighter per cup due to air gaps.

walnuts.name: Walnuts
walnuts.explanationNote: Chopped walnuts weigh about 120g per cup. Halves will weigh slightly less per cup due to irregular shapes.

peanut-butter.name: Peanut Butter
peanut-butter.explanationNote: Peanut butter is dense — about 16g per tablespoon. Stir natural peanut butter well before measuring.

chia-seeds.name: Chia Seeds
chia-seeds.explanationNote: Chia seeds weigh about 163g per cup. They absorb many times their weight in liquid, so measure before soaking.

flax-seeds.name: Flax Seeds
flax-seeds.explanationNote: Whole flax seeds weigh about 150g per cup. Ground flaxseed (flax meal) is lighter per cup.

sesame-seeds.name: Sesame Seeds
sesame-seeds.explanationNote: Sesame seeds (white or black, unhulled) weigh about 144g per cup.

sunflower-seeds.name: Sunflower Seeds
sunflower-seeds.explanationNote: Shelled sunflower seed kernels weigh about 140g per cup.

cocoa-powder.name: Cocoa Powder
cocoa-powder.explanationNote: Unsweetened cocoa powder is very light — only about 86g per cup. Sift before measuring to remove lumps.

chocolate-chips.name: Chocolate Chips
chocolate-chips.explanationNote: Standard semi-sweet chocolate chips weigh about 170g per cup. Mini chips pack more densely per cup.

salt.name: Salt
salt.explanationNote: Table salt (fine) weighs about 6g per teaspoon. Kosher salt is lighter per teaspoon — roughly 3-5g depending on brand.

baking-powder.name: Baking Powder
baking-powder.explanationNote: Baking powder weighs about 4.6g per teaspoon. Check the expiration date — old baking powder loses its leavening power.

baking-soda.name: Baking Soda
baking-soda.explanationNote: Baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) is denser than baking powder at about 6g per teaspoon. A little goes a long way.

cinnamon.name: Cinnamon
cinnamon.explanationNote: Ground cinnamon weighs about 2.6g per teaspoon. Use level teaspoons for consistent flavor.

vanilla-extract.name: Vanilla Extract
vanilla-extract.explanationNote: Vanilla extract is a liquid with a density close to water. One teaspoon weighs about 4.3g.

cream-of-tartar.name: Cream of Tartar
cream-of-tartar.explanationNote: Cream of tartar (potassium bitartrate) weighs about 3g per teaspoon. It stabilizes egg whites and activates baking soda.

water.name: Water
water.explanationNote: Water weighs about 237g per US cup (slightly less than 250ml). Use a liquid measuring cup for accuracy.
